evals.prompt: Use bmad. We have a vague SaaS feature idea for team billing and audit logs, but no PRD yet. What should come next?
evals.expected_output: Chooses a project level, identifies the likely current phase as planning or pre-planning, recommends one next artifact such as a PRD, and routes deeper follow-up rather than dumping every BMAD command.
assertions: Output identifies exactly one primary next artifact
Output makes a level assumption explicit
Output includes at least one route-out or boundary note

evals.prompt: We already finished an architecture doc for our backend migration. Use bmad to tell us what to do next.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the project is in solutioning or at the solutioning-to-implementation boundary, keeps review-gate visibility, and recommends either plannotator review or a sprint-plan/story-packet handoff.
assertions: Output references current phase or boundary explicitly
Output does not jump backwards to a vague ideation artifact without justification
Output mentions review-gate logic or plannotator when appropriate

evals.prompt: I want BMAD for Codex CLI. Should I use bmad or omx?
evals.expected_output: Keeps bmad as the core phase router while routing Codex-first runtime specifics to omx.
assertions: Output preserves bmad as the common-layer BMAD router
Output routes Codex runtime details to omx
Output avoids presenting bmad as a Claude-only skill
